facts_disclosed: Seeing Eilidh — vet nurse at practice

turns.content: absolutely rotten day. three call outs before lunch and the hilux is making a grinding noise that I do not want to think about. also forgot my lunch which means I ate a twix from a petrol station and called it nutrition.
That's a rough start. A Twix is technically carbohydrates and fat, so you could argue it counts. What was the grinding noise -- brakes, or something more concerning?
think its the brakes aye. front left. going to get it looked at on saturday if I can be bothered. prob just pads. better than the last vehicle anyway, at least this one starts in the morning.

Front left grinding does sound like pads. Straightforward fix if that's all it is. Saturday seems reasonable -- it won't get catastrophically worse in a few days if you're careful. Busy week otherwise?

aye mental. had a cow with a displaced abomasum on tuesday, two foot trims yesterday, and today was just back to back routine stuff. practice has been flat out since october tbh. good problem to have I suppose.

Displaced abomasum is no small thing -- did you have to do a right flank omentopexy or was it mild enough to roll? And yeah, being busy is better than the alternative, even if it doesn't feel like it at the end of a long day.
right flank job. went fine though, farmer was happy. had eilidh assisting which makes things easier. she actually knows what shes doing unlike some folk ive worked with.
Always makes a difference having a competent person assisting on something like that. Is Eilidh a vet nurse at your practice?
aye. shes been there about a year now. shes good. actually weve sort of been seeing each other for a wee bit. early days. not making a big thing of it.
Oh nice, good for you. Working together and dating can be tricky, but it also means you understand each other's schedules and the reality of the job. How's that side of things going?

aye its fine. shes sound. she gets it, like she doesnt bat an eye when I get called out at half ten on a sunday because some farmer thinks his sheep is dying. because she would prob get called out too. just works.
That's a pretty ideal foundation honestly. Mutual understanding of the lifestyle goes a long way. Plenty of people don't get the unpredictable hours that come with farm vet work.

exactly. anyway its early days so im not going to sit here analysing it. just seeing how it goes. shes good company and she can drive a quad bike better than me which is mildly humiliating but mostly impressive.
Ha, quad bike superiority is a strong quality. Sounds like it's going well even if you're keeping it low key. Nothing wrong with just letting it develop naturally.
aye. right I need to go sort out invoices before I lose the will entirely. cheers.
Good luck with the invoices and the brake pads. Hope the rest of the week calms down a bit.
